response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of an open-source GIS platform, Green Map work, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems engineering at Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second, but the role's core requirement of modern C++ or Rust is weakly met: his professional C++ work was a seven-month stint in 2014-2015, C++ is absent from his skills list, and his recent years are TypeScript/Node full-stack work. He has no navigation or routing systems experience, so the fit hinges on transferring DLang systems skills to C++, which is plausible but unproven.
strengths: Real geospatial domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map GIS applications
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) in DLang at Sociomantic
Strong engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, and DevOps/Kubernetes exposure
concerns: Modern C++/Rust requirement not met; professional C++ experience is brief and roughly a decade old
No navigation guidance or routing pipeline experience specifically
Recent focus is TypeScript/Node web development, far from Linux C++ data pipelines
